summary: This notebook demonstrates how to set up and run the open-weight gpt-oss:20b model locally within a Kaggle notebook environment using Ollama. It walks through installing dependencies, starting the Ollama server, downloading the model, and wrapping the local API in a reusable Python class for interactive chat. The author provides utility functions for managing conversation history, system prompts, logging, and troubleshooting, making it a practical template for running local LLMs in restricted cloud environments. Readers should learn how to bridge local model serving with the OpenAI Python SDK and manage stateful interactions programmatically.
competition_flow: Installs Ollama, downloads the gpt-oss:20b model, and provides a Python interface to interact with it locally via the OpenAI API.
insights: Local open-weight models can be served and queried in Kaggle notebooks using Ollama and the OpenAI-compatible API.
Conversation state and system prompts can be managed programmatically without relying on external commercial APIs.
Ollama provides a straightforward CLI for downloading and running large models locally in cloud environments.
